title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market Closed: Sunday, March 1, 2026 - Friday's Close Summary

sections.title: Market Pulse: Wall Street Slides on Inflation and AI Fears

items.headline: U.S. Markets Close Week Lower Amid Inflationary Pressures and AI Uncertainty
items.summary: U.S. equity markets concluded the trading week on Friday, February 28, 2026, with a significant slide across major indices. Investors reacted negatively to a surprisingly discouraging update on producer price inflation and persistent worries surrounding the artificial intelligence (AI) boom's sustainability. The overall sentiment remains cautiously bearish heading into the weekend. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Applied Optoelectronics Surges
items.summary: Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI) experienced a significant surge, closing as one of the top gainers.

items.headline: Dell Soars on Positive Outlook
items.summary: Dell Technologies ($DELL) shares climbed considerably after investors responded positively to its growth forecast.

items.headline: Sunrun Shares Decline Sharply
items.summary: Sunrun Inc. ($RUN) saw a sharp decline in its share price during Friday's session.

items.headline: Amphastar Plummets on Earnings Miss
items.summary: Amphastar Pharmaceuticals ($AMPH) shares plummeted following its Q4 2025 earnings report, which missed both profitability and revenue consensus estimates.

items.headline: Producer Price Index Surprises Higher, Fuels Rate Cut Concerns
items.summary: The January Producer Price Index (PPI) data, released on Friday, showed wholesale prices increasing by 0.8% month-over-month, significantly more than economists had predicted. This surprisingly discouraging inflation update pressured expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, contributing to the market's downturn.

items.headline: Tech Sector Slips Amid AI Volatility and Inflation Worries
items.summary: The technology-heavy NASDAQ Composite led the declines, as tech stocks experienced a downturn fueled by ongoing uncertainty regarding the artificial intelligence (AI) boom and broader inflation jitters. Nvidia ($NVDA) notably fell by 4.2%. This signals a potential shift as investors move from growth to more cyclical sectors.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Escalate, Driving Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: Reports of the U.S. launching combat operations in Iran on Saturday exacerbated geopolitical tensions, which had already contributed to rising oil prices. The price for a barrel of benchmark U.S. crude rose 2.8% to settle at US$67.02 on Friday, reflecting market concerns about potential disruptions to global oil flow.

items.headline: Q4 Earnings Season Nears Close; Retailers Show Resilience
items.summary: With over 90% of S&P 500 companies having reported Q4 earnings, the season is wrapping up. Blowout reports from tech giants like Nvidia and several retailers, including TJX, boosted Q4 EPS growth, which topped 13%. Attention now shifts to Q1 2026 guidance as trade risk returns to the forefront.
